evidence.decolonization: The lesson avoids decorative folk-mass and authenticity-cliche framing, preserves kobza/bandura/lira distinctions, treats trembita source disagreement explicitly, and separates institutional history from Soviet ideological language.
evidence.pedagogy: C1/C1+ Ukrainian prose connects material culture, sound, technique, event, institution, and modern reuse with V2 inline/workbook practice and 43 vocabulary items.
evidence.review: Claude Opus 4.8 independent review found one major process-language leak and one minor English resource-note issue; both were fixed in source, regenerated to MDX, and follow-up review returned PASS with no blockers. A later focused quote-verification pass tightened the Yanovskyi fragment to its attested order; final local gates required VESUM-clean rewrites without changing source discipline.
evidence.source_policy: Source-grounded module distinguishes instrument construction, repertoire, social role, performer status, regional usage, revival, Soviet academicization, and source confidence without invented songs, audio scenes, makers, or image identifiers; final resource links are live and gate-compatible.
